name: Barco-Newton YMCA
description: Come play pickleball at Barco-Newton YMCA in Fleming Island, FL! There are 2 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. Players have access to restrooms. Reservations are available.

name: Dye Clay YMCA
description: Dye Clay YMCA is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Orange Park, FL. There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts can be reserved. The facility includes amenities like lights. A membership is required to play.

name: Foxmeadow Recreational Park
description: Foxmeadow Recreational Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Middleburg, FL. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. Reservations are not available. The courts are free.

name: Greyhawk Amenity Center at Oakleaf Plantation
description: 2 courts with mobile net at amenity center

name: Keystone Heights Sunrise Park
description: Keystone Heights Sunrise Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Keystone Heights, FL. There is 1 outdoor acrylic court. This is a dedicated court with a permanent net and lines. The facility includes amenities like restrooms. The court is free.

name: Kingsley Lake Park
description: Kingsley Lake Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Starke, FL. There is 1 outdoor court. You’ll have to create your own lines and bring your own net. The court is free.

name: Little Rain Lake Park
description: Little Rain Lake Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Keystone Heights, FL. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free.

name: Oakleaf Village Fitness Center
description: Oakleaf Village Fitness Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Orange Park, FL. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts cannot be reserved. The courts are private. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, lights, and trainers or lessons.

name: Orange Park Sports & Recreation Park
description: Come play pickleball at Orange Park Sports & Recreation Park in Orange Park, FL! There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, and lights. Reservations are not available. The courts are free.

name: Ronnie Van Zant Memorial Park
description: Ronnie Van Zant Memorial Park is a place to play pickleball in Green Cove Springs, FL. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Amenities include restrooms. Reservations are available.

name: Tennis At Rolling Hills
description: Tennis At Rolling Hills is a place to play pickleball in Green Cove Springs, FL. There are 6 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Amenities include water, wheelchair accessibility, and lights. The courts are free. The courts can be reserved.

name: The HUB Jacksonville
description: The HUB Jacksonville is a place to play pickleball in Orange Park, FL. There are 24 concrete courts. 16 are indoors and 8 are outdoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like food, restrooms, water, youth programming, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, trainers or lessons, wheelchair accessibility, and adaptive programming. You can reserve the courts.

name: Thomas Hogans Memorial Gym
description: Come play pickleball at Thomas Hogans Memorial Gym in Green Cove Springs, FL! There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Town Center Pickleball Court
description: Requires Fleming Island resident ID. Can bring 2 guests per person with an ID. There is a security officer that looks for people without resident ID cards

name: Twin Lakes Community Park Pickleball
description:
Welcome Pickleball Players! 🏓☀️ Twin Lakes welcomes players that are 45 years of age and up, as we are a 55+ community with brand new pickleball courts! We have 4 dedicated asphalt courts with permanent lines and nets.

Our newly introduced Free Open Play Saturdays is for all skill levels and currently first come first serve. We will consider reserving the courts for serious players as we go along, but no reservations are taken at this time.

Signing our waiver is mandatory prior to playing. Please visit the office in the clubhouse any time and we will happily get you signed up! Our office hours are Tuesday-Saturday 9:30AM-5:00PM, Sunday 11:00AM-4:30PM, and we are closed on Mondays.

name: Twin Lakes Park
description: Twin Lakes Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Keystone Heights, FL. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: W. E. Varnes Park
description: Come play pickleball at W. E. Varnes Park in Orange Park, FL! There are 8 outdoor asphalt and hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. You can’t reserve the courts. Amenities include lights.
